meta.title: Transcription of the Bacillus subtilis Bacteriophage ø3T in vitro

abstractHtml: The in vitro transcription pattern of BglII-digested ø3T DNA is described. Eight BglII fragments that hybridized to in vitro transcription products were unequivocally identified. A further hybridizing region corresponding to a BglII triplet was also revealed, giving a total of nine to 11 BglII fragments. These represent 47 to 53% of the ø3T genome. Transcription was shown to initiate within BglII fragments B, G, C, H, I, F and J, implying that all of these contain at least one promoter. The relevance of these data to the construction of a cloning vector based on ø3T is discussed.
